summary: Iter20 backend testing: 25/25 tests PASS across voucher CRUD, apply/remove, targeted, expired, max_uses, percent discount, monthly leaderboard run+state, admin subscription PATCH edit, user self-renew anytime, and Iter19 regression.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2470 lines — should be split into routers (auth, subscriptions, payments, admin, leaderboard). Voucher/CMS/analytics are already routers; consider extracting the rest.
UploadReceiptInput requires payment_id in body while the URL path already contains payment_id — redundant but not a bug. Consider dropping to simplify client contract.
admin_leaderboard_run_now hard-codes force=True and ignores the documented ?force=true query param — either honor the query param or remove the misleading docstring.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates TEST_iter20_* users and TEST-prefixed voucher codes. Not cleaned up but harmless (isolated per test run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter20.py has 25 tests using parallel pytest-xdist (2 workers). Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env. Netflix service must exist with price_regular=45000. Receipt upload POST /api/me/payments/{id}/receipt requires payment_id in body (redundant with URL param). Leaderboard run-now always uses force=True regardless of query param.
rca of the issue: Two initial failures were test-script bugs — receipt upload payload was missing the required payment_id field. Fixed by adding it. All 25 tests now pass in 96s.
